evals.name: safety-standards-application
evals.prompt: You are developing flight software for a high-power rocket. Describe how you would implement the NASA Power of 10 rules, MISRA-C essentials, and BARR-C guidelines in your C codebase.
assertions: Should mention all 3 NASA Power of 10 rules: no goto, no unbounded recursion, loops with static bounds
Should mention MISRA-C rules about signedness conversions, initialization, and macro usage
Should mention BARR-C guidelines about global variables and return code checking
Should provide specific examples of how to implement each rule

evals.name: state-machine-design
evals.prompt: Design a flight state machine for a two-stage rocket with boost, coast, apogee, descent, and recovery phases. Include entry/exit actions and guarded transitions.
assertions: Should define states: pre-flight, boost, coast, apogee, descent, recovery, landed
Should include transition conditions (e.g., burnout detection for boost->coast)
Should include entry/exit actions for at least two states
Should mention guarded transitions with safety checks

evals.name: telemetry-specification
evals.prompt: Specify a LoRa-based telemetry system for a rocket that needs to transmit GPS coordinates, altitude, velocity, and sensor data at 1Hz during flight.
assertions: Should specify LoRa packet structure (preamble, sync word, header, payload, CRC)
Should mention frequency band selection based on launch location (ISM bands)
Should include FEC (Forward Error Correction) implementation
Should specify SD card data logging with timestamped files and buffered writes

evals.name: testing-strategy
evals.prompt: Define a comprehensive testing strategy for flight software including unit, integration, hardware-in-the-loop (HIL), and flight simulation tests.
assertions: Should require unit tests with mock hardware abstractions
Should require integration testing for component interfaces
Should require HIL testing with simulated inputs
Should require flight simulation end-to-end tests
Should mention specific frameworks like Unity, CMock, or Ceedling
